channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: GGML HIV Campaign Gains Momentum in Moshi, Draws Thousands at Marathon Exhibition
item.description: The GGML Kili Challenge Against HIV/AIDS exhibition booth at the Kilimanjaro Marathon 2026 in Moshi has drawn a large crowd of marathon attendees and visitors, all eager to learn about the initiative’...

item.title: Victor Osimhen Arrives in Istanbul for Medical Check-up After Arm Injury Against Liverpool
item.description: Victor Osimhen, the Nigerian striker playing for Galatasaray, has arrived in Istanbul for further medical evaluations after suffering an arm fracture during a UEFA Champions League match against Liver...

item.title: Gilas Pilipinas Women improve to 30th spot in FIBA world rankings after strong international showing
item.description: The Gilas Pilipinas Women’s basketball team has achieved a significant milestone by moving up to No. 30 in the latest FIBA world rankings, marking one of the highest positions the Philippine women’s t...

item.title: Tanzania’s Cotton-to-Clothing Strategy Revives the Textile Industry
item.description: For a long time, Tanzania, Africa’s second-largest cotton producer, has been exporting raw cotton to other nations instead of processing it locally. This approach meant losing jobs and revenue as fore...

item.title: Challenges Facing Africa in Turning Crude Oil into Domestic Fuel
item.description: Even though Africa get big oil reserves, many countries still dey depend on imported fuel. This one happen because of plenty reasons wey dey mix technical, financial, and market factors. Experts like ...

item.title: North West Boosts Vaccination Effort to Combat Foot and Mouth Disease in Livestock
item.description: The North West Department of Agriculture and Rural Development has ramped up its efforts to combat the spread of Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) in the province. Over 111,000 animals have already been va...

item.title: British couple imprisoned in Iran plead for support amid worsening conditions
item.description: Craig and Lindsay Foreman, a British couple from East Sussex, have described their imprisonment in Iran as 'life-threatening' amid escalating conflict in the region. The pair were arrested in January ...

item.title: Guided Shark Diving: Adventure, Education, and Ocean Conservation
item.description: Guided shark diving don become one of di most thrilling water sports wey dey attract tourists wey wan get unforgettable experience under water. Wey before, e be activity for professional divers or mar...

item.title: Kenya's Remittances Decline by Sh21.6 Billion in March, Reserves Remain Stable
item.description: Money sent home by Kenyans living abroad fell by Sh21.6 billion in March 2026, according to the latest figures from the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK). Total inflows dropped to Sh1.85 trillion last week,...

item.title: Global Study Reveals Widespread Human-Made Chemicals in Oceans
item.description: A groundbreaking international study has revealed that human-made chemicals are now deeply embedded within the chemistry of the world's oceans. Led by researchers from Rhodes University (South Africa)...

item.title: Two Wanted Criminals Killed in Sultan Kudarat Police Operation

item.title: NDA finalises seat-sharing for Tamil Nadu elections; BJP, PMK, and AMMK to contest selected seats
item.description: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) has confirmed its seat-sharing arrangement for the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. According to the deal, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) will contest 2...

item.title: Meningitis Outbreak in Kent: Symptoms, How it Spreads, and What You Can Do to Stay Safe
item.description: A meningitis outbreak in Kent, UK, has raised concerns after spreading rapidly, especially within student circles. The outbreak, attributed to Meningitis B (MenB), has already claimed two lives. The i...

item.title: Atlantic Canada Faces Severe Winter Storm Leading to School and Event Closures
item.description: A major winter storm swept through Atlantic Canada early on March 23, 2026, prompting numerous school closures and activity cancellations across the region. The system brought heavy snowfall, strong w...

item.title: Fuel prices expected to rise further due to Middle East conflict, supply remains steady
item.description: Fuel prices in the UK are set to rise further as a result of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, according to the Petrol Retailers Association (PRA). While wholesale diesel prices have surged, ri...

item.title: Explosion In Woro Community, Kwara State, Leaves Casualties Amid Boko Haram Fears
item.description: Early Monday morning, one strong explosion shake Woro community wey dey inside Kaiama Local Government Area for Kwara State. Local people tell SaharaReporters say one car run over one IED wey Boko Har...

item.title: China's Vice President Han Zheng on Official Visit to Kenya
item.description: China's Vice President Han Zheng arrived in Nairobi on Sunday for a four-day official visit aimed at strengthening the long-standing ties between Kenya and China. The visit is focused on enhancing coo...

item.title: Australian Sharemarket Suffers $300 Billion Loss Amid Iran-US Tensions
item.description: The Australian sharemarket has taken a significant hit, with over $300 billion wiped off its value since the Trump administration's missile strikes on Iran. Investor sentiment has been shaken by escal...

item.title: Long-term Caspase-2 inhibition in liver may increase cancer risk despite short-term benefits
item.description: A recent study from the University of Adelaide reveals potential long-term risks associated with blocking the enzyme Caspase-2, a strategy previously thought to help treat fatty liver disease. While C...

item.title: GP Discusses Pay, Health Advice, and the Realities of Modern Practice
item.description: Dr George Hawche, a GP based in west London, shares insights from his career in general practice, offering advice on health, lifestyle, and the NHS. He explains that salaried GPs earn between £80,000 ...
